memory.title: Global Memory
memory.description: When enabled, memories are extracted/updated in the background after chats, and can be searched on demand during conversations.
memory.rule1: Only stores long-term, user-related facts (e.g., identity, preferences, lasting constraints).
memory.rule2: It won’t save one-off questions, general knowledge, or web search results.
memory.rule3: You can also add / edit / delete memories manually below.
memory.enableDesc: You also need to enable memory in the assistant settings.
memory.notConfiguredDesc: Please select an Embedding model and a Memory LLM model first.
memory.invalidSelectionTitle: Selected memory models are no longer available
memory.invalidSelectionDesc: The current {{models}} selection is no longer available. Please choose a valid model again.
memory.invalidEmbeddingModel: The selected embedding model is no longer available. Please choose another one.
memory.embeddingModelDesc: Used for vectorization and similarity search (must be an embedding model).
memory.llmModelDesc: Used to extract and merge memories from conversations.
memory.rerankModelDesc: Optional: reranks Top-K vector results by semantic relevance (must be a rerank model).
memory.topKDesc: Maximum memories to retrieve per chat.
memory.addPlaceholder: Add a memory, e.g. my name is..., I prefer..., I dislike...
memory.editDesc: Updating a memory will regenerate its embedding for future search.
memory.configureEmbeddingFirst: Please configure an Embedding model first
memory.deleteConfirmDesc: This deletes the memory: "{{content}}". Future chats will no longer retrieve it.
memory.clearConfirmDesc: After clearing, future chats will no longer retrieve any saved memories.
